article.title: How Job Design for Disability Improves Work for Everyone

harvestedTerms.summary: MIT Sloan Management Review article arguing that disability-inclusive job design generates process innovations that benefit all workers — drawing on the 'curb cut effect' as a model for how accommodations become universal improvements. The article is management-focused, not AI-focused, with only incidental mentions of AI-adjacent technologies (voice recognition, ergonomic tools). It contains no substantive AI terminology.

article.title: GDS weighs in on the NHS’s decision to retreat from Open Source

harvestedTerms.summary: Simon Willison links to GDS's public guidance on AI, open code, and vulnerability risk in the public sector, framing it as a rebuke to the NHS's decision to close open-source repositories after vulnerabilities were reported via Project Glasswing. The GDS recommends keeping code open by default, arguing closure adds costs and reduces scrutiny.

article.title: Revamped Siri will reportedly offer auto-deleting chats

harvestedTerms.summary: Short news article reporting that Apple's revamped Siri in iOS 27 will feature auto-deleting chat histories and memory restrictions as a privacy differentiator, contrasting with competitors' persistent memory and personalization approaches.

article.title: Should Your Subscription Business Use Auto-Renew?

harvestedTerms.summary: HBR article on auto-renewal strategy in subscription businesses. No meaningful AI terminology present — the article is about subscription business models, customer retention, and recurring revenue strategy, with only an incidental author affiliation mention of AI in business and society.
